name: Gold
summary: Day 67 Tuesday. GOLD $4,558.70 (+0.9% Tue 1D, -2.1% 1M, -6.2% 3M) — partial recovery after May 4 dip below $4,600 watching trigger; gave back the May 1 push to $4,644.50. The May 3 brief noted Day 10 of inflation-regime call ('REINFORCED') with Powell 'it hasn't even peaked yet' framing; May 4-5 events partially test that framing — kinetic restart did NOT produce a clean safe-haven bid in gold. US 10Y +1.6% Tue to 4.45% (5-week high) — real yields competing harder. DXY 98.52 (+0.1%) — firm. The 'INR ₹95.00+ sustained' partial regime-shift trigger (which was specified as one path to gold-rally regime change) IS NOW ACTIVATED Day 2 — Tue ₹95.34 — but gold's response is muted (+0.9%), suggesting the regime is still INFLATION-RISK DOMINANT not yet FINANCIAL-SYSTEM-RISK. Crowded COMEX positioning persists. The watching-trigger 'Gulf-exposed bank reports unexpected Q1 loss' has NOT activated. The INFLATION regime call holds. For investor: gold tracks DXY/real-yields, not crisis-beta — kinetic-restart day proved this with $25 round-trip but no regime change.

current.regime: INFLATION-RISK DOMINANT — kinetic-restart May 4 produced muted +0.9% gold recovery; INR ₹95+ activation Day 2 produced no regime-shift bid; gold-as-FINANCIAL-SYSTEM-RISK regime NOT yet triggered.
current.watching_threshold: $4,600 watching trigger; Mon May 4 close ~$4,517 was below — would need 3+ sessions sustained above $4,600 for regime-shift confirmation.

active_triggers.condition: US 10Y real yield falls 15bps in 3 sessions (inflation-regime trigger — more predictive than crisis-escalation)
Gulf-exposed bank reports unexpected Q1 loss OR Hormuz-action triggers CEF forced closure → credit stress routes into gold
